info.title: Consumer Product Recalls API
info.description:
The consumer product recalls data on this site are also available via API that is available for good-faith use. It's open and read-only: no key, GET requests only, and open CORS. It's limited to 60 requests a minute per IP, and results are cursor-paginated. The base URL is `https://api.consumer-product-recalls.info`.

A few things are worth knowing first. A recall's `is_active` flag is `null` rather than false for CPSC and NHTSA, since neither tracks an open or closed status. `classification` uses each agency's own severity scale, so the codes don't translate across agencies. UPC search matches a whole recall, not a single product. And search is exact, so a typo turns up nothing.

**Common lookups**

- By product name: `GET /products/search?q=`
- By UPC barcode: `GET /products/search?upc=`
- A vehicle or boat by identifier: `GET /products/search?model=` or `?hin=`
- A single recall: `GET /recalls/{source}/{recall_id}`

get.description: Liveness: process is up. No DB touch, so the Docker/Fly probe never wakes Neon.

get.summary: Readiness probe: verifies the database connection.
get.description: Readiness: SELECT 1 to Neon; a cold DB becomes 503 + Retry-After via the error handler.

get.summary: List recalls, newest first, with filters and pagination.
get.description: Recalls across all five agencies, newest first (by when each was most recently published or updated), with cursor pagination. Pass a page's `next_cursor` back as `cursor` for the next page. Filters combine with AND across fields; the categorical ones (`source`, `classification`, `lifecycle_status`, `distribution_scope`, `distribution_state`, `distribution_country`) accept multiple values: repeat the param or comma-separate them (`?source=CPSC,FDA`) to match any of them within that field. Each filter notes its own caveats below. The total count is omitted unless you pass `with_total=true`.

schema.description: Issuing agency; repeat or comma-separate to match any of several.
schema.title: Source

parameters.description: Issuing agency; repeat or comma-separate to match any of several.

parameters.name: is_active

schema.description: CPSC and NHTSA have no open/closed status, so they're null and match neither true nor false.

get.summary: Search recalls by keyword, with the same filters.
get.description: Keyword search over recalls, full-text across each recall's title, product names, firm, and narrative, ranked by relevance (`rank`). Matches whole words and prefixes only; no fuzzy or typo search. Unlike `/products/search` (which is product-level and also does identifier and UPC lookups via `?upc=`), this returns one row per recall, each linking to its detail route. All `/recalls` filters apply here too.

get.summary: Fetch one recall's full record by agency and recall id.
get.description: The full record for one recall, identified by its issuing agency and that agency's own recall id (e.g. `CPSC/24-001`). The agency name is case-insensitive. A couple of field notes: `classification`, `risk_level`, and `lifecycle_status` use each agency's own vocabulary; `is_active` is `null` for CPSC and NHTSA; and `distribution_states` is the agency's free-text prose, separate from the parsed `distribution_state_codes`.

parameters.name: source

schema.description: Issuing agency (accepted case-insensitively).
schema.title: Source

parameters.description: Issuing agency (accepted case-insensitively).

parameters.name: recall_id

schema.description: The agency-native recall id.
schema.title: Recall Id

parameters.description: The agency-native recall id.

get.summary: Search recalled products by keyword (full-text) or exact identifier.
get.description: Search recalled products ("is my product recalled?"). Keyword (`q`) is a full-text search over product name, description, recall title, and firm, ranked by relevance (`rank`); whole words and prefixes only, no fuzzy or typo search. `hin` and `model` are exact-match lookups. `upc` matches at the recall level, so each hit carries `upc_is_recall_level: true`; a miss means no recall lists that UPC, not that the product is safe. Supply at least one of `q`, `hin`, `model`, or `upc` (otherwise 422); if several are given, `q` wins, then `hin`/`model`, then `upc`. `source` narrows any of these and accepts multiple values (repeat or comma-separate).

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Keywords to search for.
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Keywords to search for.

parameters.name: hin

schema.description: Exact Hull ID (USCG boats).
schema.title: Hin

parameters.description: Exact Hull ID (USCG boats).

parameters.name: model

schema.description: Exact product model.
schema.title: Model

parameters.description: Exact product model.

parameters.name: upc

schema.description: Look up recalled products by 12-digit UPC barcode. Matches at the recall level. UPC data is sparse and comes only from CPSC (about 5% of CPSC recalls; absent for FDA, USDA, NHTSA, and USCG). A miss means no recall lists that UPC, **not** that the product is safe.
schema.title: Upc

parameters.description: Look up recalled products by 12-digit UPC barcode. Matches at the recall level. UPC data is sparse and comes only from CPSC (about 5% of CPSC recalls; absent for FDA, USDA, NHTSA, and USCG). A miss means no recall lists that UPC, **not** that the product is safe.

parameters.name: source

schema.description: Optional agency filter; repeat/comma-separate to match any of several.
schema.title: Source

parameters.description: Optional agency filter; repeat/comma-separate to match any of several.

schema.description: Page size (max 100).
schema.title: Limit

schema.title: With Total

get.summary: Fetch a firm profile with its agency registration records.
get.description: A single firm, merged across agencies, so a maker that appears under several (e.g. Honda under NHTSA and USCG) shows up as one profile. Includes recall counts, the per-agency breakdown (`recalls_by_source`), name variants, and agency registration records: `firm_usda_attributes` (USDA establishments), `firm_uscg_attributes` (USCG boat builders), and `firm_fda_attributes` (FDA-registered firms). Each set has a different shape and any may be empty; CPSC and NHTSA contribute none. `first_recall_at` and `last_recall_at` are null for a firm with no matched recalls. The id is opaque; get it from a recall's `firms[].firm_id`.

parameters.name: firm_id

schema.description: Opaque canonical firm id (md5 cluster id).
schema.title: Firm Id

parameters.description: Opaque canonical firm id (md5 cluster id).

get.summary: Headline KPIs: total recalls, distinct firms, sources covered, last gold rebuild.
Recall counts per period (month/week/year) per source + 'ALL' rollup.
get.description: Recall counts per period (month, week, or year) for each agency, plus an `ALL` rollup. Periods are dated by when each recall was first announced, falling back to its publish date when no announcement date is on record, so counts can differ from a publish-date view.

parameters.name: grain

schema.description: Time grain: month | week | year.

parameters.description: Time grain: month | week | year.

parameters.name: source

schema.description: Filter to one agency, or `ALL` for the all-agency rollup where available. Omit to get every row; an agency with no data for this stat returns an empty list.
schema.title: Source

parameters.description: Filter to one agency, or `ALL` for the all-agency rollup where available. Omit to get every row; an agency with no data for this stat returns an empty list.

get.summary: Units recalled per agency and unit type, by month (not comparable across agencies).
get.description: Units recalled per agency and unit type, by month. Units come in incomparable kinds (counts, weight, volume), so never add them across kinds or agencies. Months are dated by when each recall was first announced (or its publish date when none is on record).

parameters.name: source

schema.description: Filter to one agency, or `ALL` for the all-agency rollup where available. Omit to get every row; an agency with no data for this stat returns an empty list.
schema.title: Source

parameters.description: Filter to one agency, or `ALL` for the all-agency rollup where available. Omit to get every row; an agency with no data for this stat returns an empty list.

classification.title: Classification
classification.description: Recall severity in each agency's own scale (FDA: 1/2/3, NC = Not Yet Classified; USDA: Class I/II/III, Public Health Alert; USCG: H/L/M/S). Not comparable across agencies. ⚠ USCG's H/L/M/S are passed through from the USCG directory, but their official meaning is not publicly documented (the public USCG recall index shows no severity, and 33 CFR 179 defines none), so do not assume an ordered scale. Best current guess, pending confirmation from USCG: H/M/L roughly map to High/Medium/Low, and S is unverified. Sources: FDA, USDA, USCG (null for CPSC/NHTSA).

risk_level.title: Risk Level
risk_level.description: USDA-only health-risk label; null for the other agencies.

ClassificationCount.title: ClassificationCount
ClassificationCount.description: Recall counts by each agency's own classification and risk level, plus an ``ALL`` rollup.

recall_count.title: Recall Count
recall_count.description: Recalls distributed to this country. A recall sold in the US and abroad counts once per country, so per-country counts add up to more than the total.

CountryCount.title: CountryCount
CountryCount.description: Per-country recall counts for distribution (FDA and USDA), plus an ``ALL`` rollup.

DbHealth.title: DbHealth
DbHealth.description: Readiness body, returned only when the database is reachable.

DistributionScope.title: DistributionScope
DistributionScope.description: How widely a product was distributed: Nationwide, Regional, International, or Unspecified.

FdaAttributes.title: FdaAttributes
FdaAttributes.description: An FDA registration record for the firm.

event_count.title: Event Count
event_count.description: Total distinct recalls for the firm (all agencies).

active_recalls.title: Active Recalls
active_recalls.description: Currently active recalls (FDA, USDA, USCG only).

product_count.title: Product Count
product_count.description: Distinct recalled products for this firm.

event_count_rank.title: Event Count Rank
event_count_rank.description: Rank by total recalls (1 = most recalled).

FirmLeaderRow.title: FirmLeaderRow
FirmLeaderRow.description: A row of the most-recalled-firms leaderboard.

firm_id.title: Firm Id
firm_id.description: Opaque id for this firm (the merged cross-agency entity). One per firm. Sources: derived (all five agencies contribute names).

canonical_name.title: Canonical Name
canonical_name.description: The firm's display name: its resolved name, or a representative raw name when it stands alone. Not an authoritative legal name. Sources: all five.

normalized_name.title: Normalized Name
normalized_name.description: An upper-cased, trimmed form of the firm's name for case-insensitive lookup. Not unique (`firm_id` is the key). Sources: all five.

observed_names.title: Observed Names
observed_names.description: All the distinct raw spellings of this firm's name that were merged together. Always has at least one. Sources: all five.

observed_company_ids.title: Observed Company Ids
observed_company_ids.description: The firm's structured identifiers: FDA registration numbers, USDA establishment numbers, and USCG manufacturer codes. Empty for firms seen only through CPSC or NHTSA, which carry no usable id. Sources: FDA, USDA, USCG.

alternate_names.title: Alternate Names
alternate_names.description: Brand or 'doing business as' aliases for the firm (e.g. 'John Deere' for 'Deere & Company'). Empty when the firm has none. Sources: derived (not a per-agency field).

total_recalls.title: Total Recalls
total_recalls.description: Total distinct recalls this firm is linked to, across every agency it appears in (multiple roles on one recall count once). Always present. Sources: all five.

active_recalls.title: Active Recalls
active_recalls.description: How many of the firm's recalls are currently active. Only FDA, USDA, and USCG recalls can be active; CPSC and NHTSA have no status and never count. Always present (0 if none). Sources: FDA, USDA, USCG.

first_recall_at.title: First Recall At
first_recall_at.description: The firm's earliest recall date, by when each recall was first announced (or its publish date when no announcement date exists). Null only for a firm with no linked recall. Sources: all five.

last_recall_at.title: Last Recall At
last_recall_at.description: The firm's most recent recall date, on the same basis as `first_recall_at`. Null only for a firm with no linked recall. Sources: all five.

roles.title: Roles
roles.description: The distinct roles this firm has played: manufacturer, importer, distributor (CPSC), establishment (FDA/USDA), filer or manufacturer (NHTSA), manufacturer (USCG). Sources: all five.

recalls_by_source.title: Recalls By Source
recalls_by_source.description: A map of agency to the firm's recall count (e.g. {'NHTSA': 12, 'CPSC': 3}). Only agencies where the firm has at least one recall appear, and the values sum to `total_recalls`. Sources: all five.

distinct_products.title: Distinct Products
distinct_products.description: Total distinct recalled products across all this firm's recalls, in any role. This is a per-firm tally, so a product on a multi-firm recall is counted under each firm. Never null. Sources: all five.

firm_usda_attributes.title: Firm Usda Attributes
firm_usda_attributes.description: USDA establishment records, one per matched establishment (name, address, regulatory details, grant dates). Empty for non-USDA firms. Sources: USDA only.

firm_uscg_attributes.title: Firm Uscg Attributes
firm_uscg_attributes.description: USCG boat-builder records, one per manufacturer code the firm is registered under (company name, address, status, ownership history). Empty for non-USCG firms. Sources: USCG only.

firm_fda_attributes.title: Firm Fda Attributes
firm_fda_attributes.description: FDA establishment records, one per FDA registration the firm is grouped under (legal name, address, succession signal). Empty for non-FDA firms. Sources: FDA only.

FirmProfile.title: FirmProfile
FirmProfile.description: A single firm merged across agencies: names, recall stats, and registration records.

role.title: Role
role.description: Role this firm played on the recall (manufacturer/importer/distributor for CPSC, establishment for FDA/USDA, filer/manufacturer for NHTSA, manufacturer for USCG).

match_confidence.title: Match Confidence
match_confidence.description: How this firm was matched to the recall (e.g. exact_name, fei_exact).

FirmRef.title: FirmRef
FirmRef.description: One firm tied to a recall; ``firm_id`` links to ``GET /firms/{firm_id}``.

GeographyBasis.title: GeographyBasis
GeographyBasis.description: The two geography views for ``/stats/by-geography`` (they answer different questions).

geography_basis.title: Geography Basis
geography_basis.description: 'distribution' (where the product went; FDA/USDA) or 'firm_registration' (where the firm is registered; USDA/USCG/FDA). These answer different questions and are not interchangeable.

state_code.title: State Code
state_code.description: Two-letter US state or territory code.

recall_count.title: Recall Count
recall_count.description: Recalls touching this state. A recall is counted in every state it touches, so per-state counts add up to more than the total.

GeographyCount.title: GeographyCount
GeographyCount.description: Per-state recall counts, by distribution or firm-registration, plus an ``ALL`` rollup.

Grain.title: Grain
Grain.description: Time bucket for ``/stats/recalls-by-period``: month, week, or year.

event_count_year_ago.title: Event Count Year Ago
event_count_year_ago.description: The count 12 months earlier (null for the first year).

yoy_pct_change.title: Yoy Pct Change
yoy_pct_change.description: Year-over-year % change vs 12 months ago (null when undefined).

MonthlyTrendPoint.title: MonthlyTrendPoint
MonthlyTrendPoint.description: One month of an agency's recall trend, with rolling averages and year-over-year change.

period.title: Period
period.description: Start of the period (first of the month, the Monday of the week, or Jan 1).

source.title: Source
source.description: Agency, or `ALL` for the all-agency rollup.

event_count.title: Event Count
event_count.description: Number of distinct recalls in this period for this agency.

PeriodCount.title: PeriodCount
PeriodCount.description: A recall count for one period and agency (``/stats/recalls-by-period``).

recall_product_id.title: Recall Product Id
recall_product_id.description: Stable, opaque id for one recalled product line (unique, never null). Also the pagination cursor anchor. Sources: all five.

recall_event_id.title: Recall Event Id
recall_event_id.description: Id of the parent recall event (links to `GET /recalls/{source}/{recall_id}`). Several products can share one recall for CPSC, FDA, and NHTSA; one-to-one for USDA and USCG. Sources: all five.

source.description: The issuing agency: CPSC, FDA, USDA, NHTSA, or USCG. Always present.

source_recall_id.title: Source Recall Id
source_recall_id.description: Each agency's own recall identifier, paired with `source` for the public key. For CPSC, USDA, NHTSA, and USCG it is recall-level; for FDA it is the product id, and the FDA recall id is in `recall_event_id`. Always present.

product_name.title: Product Name
product_name.description: The product's name. Varies by agency: CPSC product name, USCG boat model name, FDA product description, USDA recall title, NHTSA component description. May be null for some rows. Sources: all five.

product_description.title: Product Description
product_description.description: A free-text description of the product. Varies by agency: FDA repeats the product name, USDA gives a product-items blob (about 40% null), NHTSA the component description, USCG a short defect note (about 25 characters). Sources: FDA, USDA, NHTSA, USCG (null for CPSC, which has none).

model.title: Model
model.description: Product model identifier, used by the `model` exact-match lookup. Populated only for NHTSA (e.g. 'F-150'); null for the others (USCG's boat name is in `product_name`). Sources: NHTSA only.

type.title: Type
type.description: Each agency's own product category code or label (not standardized across agencies): FDA commodity (Devices, Food, Drugs, Veterinary, Biologics, Cosmetics); USDA processing category; NHTSA recall-type code (V/T/E/C/I/X); USCG a numeric boat-type code; CPSC a free-text product type. Compare only within one agency; null where the agency provides no type. Sources: all five.

model_year.title: Model Year
model_year.description: Model year of the recalled item, as text. Populated only for NHTSA and USCG (varied formats, about 32% null for USCG); null for CPSC, FDA, and USDA. Kept as text because USCG values are not always numeric. Sources: NHTSA, USCG.

hin.title: Hin
hin.description: USCG Hull Identification Number for the recalled boat (the boating equivalent of a VIN). USCG only; only about 54% of USCG products carry a real HIN. Sources: USCG only.

recall_title.title: Recall Title
recall_title.description: Headline of the recall this product belongs to. CPSC and USDA provide one directly; for FDA, NHTSA, and USCG it is built from the recall id and firm or model name. Always present. Sources: all five.

classification.title: Classification
classification.description: Recall severity in each agency's own scale (FDA: 1/2/3, NC = Not Yet Classified; USDA: Class I/II/III, Public Health Alert; USCG: H/L/M/S). Not comparable across agencies. ⚠ USCG's H/L/M/S are passed through from the USCG directory, but their official meaning is not publicly documented (the public USCG recall index shows no severity, and 33 CFR 179 defines none), so do not assume an ordered scale. Best current guess, pending confirmation from USCG: H/M/L roughly map to High/Medium/Low, and S is unverified. Sources: FDA, USDA, USCG (null for CPSC/NHTSA).

risk_level.title: Risk Level
risk_level.description: USDA's health-risk label, mapping directly to its classification (e.g. 'High - Class I'). Sources: USDA only (null for the others).

published_at.title: Published At
published_at.description: When the recall was last published or updated. Always present, and the default sort key. Sources: all five.

url.title: Url
url.description: Agency detail-page URL for the recall. Sources: CPSC, USDA, USCG (null for FDA/NHTSA).

is_active.title: Is Active
is_active.description: Whether the recall is still active (from the FDA, USDA, or USCG status). Null for CPSC and NHTSA. Sources: FDA, USDA, USCG.

firm_name.title: Firm Name
firm_name.description: The recall's main firm, chosen by role priority (manufacturer, then establishment, filer, importer, distributor). May be null when no firm is matched. Sources: all five.

recall_product_upcs.title: Recall Product Upcs
recall_product_upcs.description: Recall-level UPC codes (shared across the whole recall and repeated on each product row; this is what UPC search matches). Empty list when absent. Populated only for CPSC and sparse there (about 5% of CPSC recalls); empty for the others. Sources: CPSC only.

rank.title: Rank
rank.description: Search relevance score (higher is more relevant). Present only on the keyword (`q`) path; null for `hin`, `model`, and `upc` lookups. Scores are not comparable between queries. Computed per request. Applies to all sources.

upc_is_recall_level.title: Upc Is Recall Level
upc_is_recall_level.description: Always true. A reminder that UPC search matches at the recall level (currently CPSC-sourced and sparse), not at the individual-product level. Applies to all sources.

recall_event_id.title: Recall Event Id
recall_event_id.description: Stable, opaque id for one recall event; it does not change between data refreshes. This is not the agency's own recall number. For the human-facing key, pair `source` with `source_recall_id`. Sources: all five.

source.description: The issuing agency: CPSC, FDA, USDA, NHTSA, or USCG. Always present.

source_recall_id.title: Source Recall Id
source_recall_id.description: Each agency's own recall identifier; its form varies by agency (e.g. CPSC recall number, FDA event id, USDA's DDD-YYYY number, NHTSA campaign number, USCG recall number). Pair with `source` for a globally unique key. Always present.

title.title: Title
title.description: The recall's headline. CPSC and USDA provide one directly; for FDA, NHTSA, and USCG (which have none) it is built from the recall id and the firm or model name. Effectively always present.

url.title: Url
url.description: Link to the recall's public detail page. Sources: CPSC, USDA, USCG (null for FDA and NHTSA, which don't publish a per-recall page).

announced_at.title: Announced At
announced_at.description: When the recall was first announced or initiated. Null for about 20 FDA recalls that have no reliable announcement date; use `published_at` when you need a date that is always present. Sources: all five.

published_at.title: Published At
published_at.description: When the recall was last published or updated. Always present, and the key used for sorting and pagination (unlike `announced_at`, which can be null). For NHTSA this is really a record-creation date, since NHTSA does not publish a last-modified date. Sources: all five.

classification.title: Classification
classification.description: Recall severity in each agency's own scale (FDA: 1/2/3, NC = Not Yet Classified; USDA: Class I/II/III, Public Health Alert; USCG: H/L/M/S). Not comparable across agencies. ⚠ USCG's H/L/M/S are passed through from the USCG directory, but their official meaning is not publicly documented (the public USCG recall index shows no severity, and 33 CFR 179 defines none), so do not assume an ordered scale. Best current guess, pending confirmation from USCG: H/M/L roughly map to High/Medium/Low, and S is unverified. Sources: FDA, USDA, USCG (null for CPSC/NHTSA).

risk_level.title: Risk Level
risk_level.description: USDA's health-risk label, which maps directly to its classification (e.g. 'High - Class I', 'Low - Class II', 'Marginal - Class III', 'Public Health Alert'). Sources: USDA only (null for the others).

lifecycle_status.title: Lifecycle Status
lifecycle_status.description: The recall's status in each agency's own words (FDA: Ongoing/Completed/Terminated; USDA: Active Recall/Closed Recall/Public Health Alert; USCG: Open/Closed). Not standardized across agencies; see `is_active` for a single yes/no. Sources: FDA, USDA, USCG (null for CPSC/NHTSA).

is_active.title: Is Active
is_active.description: Whether the recall is still active, based on each agency's status (a USDA Public Health Alert counts as active). Null for CPSC and NHTSA, which don't track a status, so they match neither true nor false. Sources: FDA, USDA, USCG.

reason_category.title: Reason Category
reason_category.description: USDA's categorized recall reasons, comma-joined (e.g. 'Unreported Allergens, Misbranding'). Sources: USDA only; for the other agencies the reason is free text in `recall_reason`.

distribution_scope.title: Distribution Scope
distribution_scope.description: How widely the product was distributed, always present: Nationwide, International, Regional, or Unspecified. Derived from distribution text for FDA and USDA; CPSC and USCG default to Unspecified, NHTSA to Nationwide. Sources: all five.

primary_firm_name.title: Primary Firm Name
primary_firm_name.description: The recall's main firm, chosen by role priority (manufacturer, then establishment, filer, importer, distributor, then alphabetical). Null only when no firm could be matched. Sources: all five.

firm_count.title: Firm Count
firm_count.description: Number of distinct firms linked to this recall across all roles. A firm with several roles counts once, so this can be less than the number of entries in `firms`. 0 when none matched. Sources: all five.

product_count.title: Product Count
product_count.description: Number of distinct products on this recall. CPSC, FDA, and NHTSA can have several; USDA and USCG always have one. Never null. Sources: all five.

has_been_edited.title: Has Been Edited
has_been_edited.description: True if the pipeline has spotted at least one meaningful change to a tracked field (recall reason, classification, status, title, or termination date) since it began tracking this recall. It is evidence of an observed edit, not an official agency amendment, and it carries no date. Present for all sources.

recall_reason.title: Recall Reason
recall_reason.description: The recall or defect narrative, in free text. What it contains varies by agency (CPSC description, FDA reason for recall, USDA summary, NHTSA defect summary, USCG short problem note); USDA may contain HTML and USCG is truncated to about 25 characters. Sources: all five.

corrective_action.title: Corrective Action
corrective_action.description: What the manufacturer and consumer should do, in free text. Sources: NHTSA only (null for the others).

consequence_of_defect.title: Consequence Of Defect
consequence_of_defect.description: What can happen if the defect is not fixed, in free text. Sources: NHTSA only (null for the others).

distribution_states.title: Distribution States
distribution_states.description: USDA's distribution states as a plain comma-joined string (e.g. 'Nationwide' or 'Arizona, California'). For parsed two-letter codes use `distribution_state_codes` instead. Sources: USDA only (null for the others).

distribution_state_codes.title: Distribution State Codes
distribution_state_codes.description: Two-letter US state and territory codes for where the product was distributed. Null when no geography could be parsed; an empty list means a foreign-only recall. Parsed conservatively, so a missing code is not proof the product was not sold there. Sources: FDA, USDA (null for CPSC/NHTSA/USCG).

distribution_country_codes.title: Distribution Country Codes
distribution_country_codes.description: Two-letter codes for the foreign countries the product was distributed to (the US is excluded by design; for US geography use `distribution_state_codes`). Null when no geography could be parsed; an empty list means a domestic-only recall. Sources: FDA (null for CPSC, USDA, NHTSA, and USCG; USDA currently provides states only).

hazards.title: Hazards
hazards.description: CPSC's structured hazard list (each entry has a free-text name; the category fields are empty at the source). Sources: CPSC only (null for the others). NHTSA's harm description is in `consequence_of_defect` instead.

product_upcs.title: Product Upcs
product_upcs.description: Recall-level UPC codes (empty list when absent). Sources: CPSC only and sparse (about 5% of CPSC recalls); empty for FDA, USDA, NHTSA, and USCG.

product_names.title: Product Names
product_names.description: Distinct product names, de-duplicated (never null; empty list when none). What this means varies by agency: CPSC product name, USCG boat model name, FDA product description, USDA recall title, NHTSA component description. Sources: all five.

models.title: Models
models.description: Distinct product model identifiers, de-duplicated (never null; empty list when none). Populated only for NHTSA (e.g. 'F-150'); always empty for the others. Sources: NHTSA only.

hins.title: Hins
hins.description: Distinct USCG Hull Identification Numbers, de-duplicated (the boating equivalent of a VIN; never null, empty list when none). USCG only, and only about 54% of USCG recalls carry a real HIN. Sources: USCG only.

firms.title: Firms
firms.description: All firms tied to this recall, one entry per firm-role, ordered by role then name. A firm with several roles appears more than once, so this can be longer than `firm_count`. Always a list (possibly empty), never null. Sources: all five.

RecallDetail.title: RecallDetail
RecallDetail.description: A recall's full record: list fields, narrative, geography, lifecycle, and related lists.

recall_event_id.title: Recall Event Id
recall_event_id.description: Stable, opaque id for one recall event; it does not change between data refreshes. This is not the agency's own recall number. For the human-facing key, pair `source` with `source_recall_id`. Sources: all five.

source.description: The issuing agency: CPSC, FDA, USDA, NHTSA, or USCG. Always present.

source_recall_id.title: Source Recall Id
source_recall_id.description: Each agency's own recall identifier; its form varies by agency (e.g. CPSC recall number, FDA event id, USDA's DDD-YYYY number, NHTSA campaign number, USCG recall number). Pair with `source` for a globally unique key. Always present.

title.title: Title
title.description: The recall's headline. CPSC and USDA provide one directly; for FDA, NHTSA, and USCG (which have none) it is built from the recall id and the firm or model name. Effectively always present.

url.title: Url
url.description: Link to the recall's public detail page. Sources: CPSC, USDA, USCG (null for FDA and NHTSA, which don't publish a per-recall page).

announced_at.title: Announced At
announced_at.description: When the recall was first announced or initiated. Null for about 20 FDA recalls that have no reliable announcement date; use `published_at` when you need a date that is always present. Sources: all five.

published_at.title: Published At
published_at.description: When the recall was last published or updated. Always present, and the key used for sorting and pagination (unlike `announced_at`, which can be null). For NHTSA this is really a record-creation date, since NHTSA does not publish a last-modified date. Sources: all five.

classification.title: Classification
classification.description: Recall severity in each agency's own scale (FDA: 1/2/3, NC = Not Yet Classified; USDA: Class I/II/III, Public Health Alert; USCG: H/L/M/S). Not comparable across agencies. ⚠ USCG's H/L/M/S are passed through from the USCG directory, but their official meaning is not publicly documented (the public USCG recall index shows no severity, and 33 CFR 179 defines none), so do not assume an ordered scale. Best current guess, pending confirmation from USCG: H/M/L roughly map to High/Medium/Low, and S is unverified. Sources: FDA, USDA, USCG (null for CPSC/NHTSA).

risk_level.title: Risk Level
risk_level.description: USDA's health-risk label, which maps directly to its classification (e.g. 'High - Class I', 'Low - Class II', 'Marginal - Class III', 'Public Health Alert'). Sources: USDA only (null for the others).

lifecycle_status.title: Lifecycle Status
lifecycle_status.description: The recall's status in each agency's own words (FDA: Ongoing/Completed/Terminated; USDA: Active Recall/Closed Recall/Public Health Alert; USCG: Open/Closed). Not standardized across agencies; see `is_active` for a single yes/no. Sources: FDA, USDA, USCG (null for CPSC/NHTSA).

is_active.title: Is Active
is_active.description: Whether the recall is still active, based on each agency's status (a USDA Public Health Alert counts as active). Null for CPSC and NHTSA, which don't track a status, so they match neither true nor false. Sources: FDA, USDA, USCG.

reason_category.title: Reason Category
reason_category.description: USDA's categorized recall reasons, comma-joined (e.g. 'Unreported Allergens, Misbranding'). Sources: USDA only; for the other agencies the reason is free text in `recall_reason`.

distribution_scope.title: Distribution Scope
distribution_scope.description: How widely the product was distributed, always present: Nationwide, International, Regional, or Unspecified. Derived from distribution text for FDA and USDA; CPSC and USCG default to Unspecified, NHTSA to Nationwide. Sources: all five.

primary_firm_name.title: Primary Firm Name
primary_firm_name.description: The recall's main firm, chosen by role priority (manufacturer, then establishment, filer, importer, distributor, then alphabetical). Null only when no firm could be matched. Sources: all five.

firm_count.title: Firm Count
firm_count.description: Number of distinct firms linked to this recall across all roles. A firm with several roles counts once, so this can be less than the number of entries in `firms`. 0 when none matched. Sources: all five.

product_count.title: Product Count
product_count.description: Number of distinct products on this recall. CPSC, FDA, and NHTSA can have several; USDA and USCG always have one. Never null. Sources: all five.

has_been_edited.title: Has Been Edited
has_been_edited.description: True if the pipeline has spotted at least one meaningful change to a tracked field (recall reason, classification, status, title, or termination date) since it began tracking this recall. It is evidence of an observed edit, not an official agency amendment, and it carries no date. Present for all sources.

rank.title: Rank
rank.description: Search relevance score (higher is more relevant), weighted toward title, then brand, cause, and harm. Scores are not comparable between different queries. Computed per request; present only on `/recalls/search`.

RecallSearchHit.title: RecallSearchHit
RecallSearchHit.description: A recall list item plus a search relevance ``rank`` (from ``GET /recalls/search``).

recall_event_id.title: Recall Event Id
recall_event_id.description: Stable, opaque id for one recall event; it does not change between data refreshes. This is not the agency's own recall number. For the human-facing key, pair `source` with `source_recall_id`. Sources: all five.

source.description: The issuing agency: CPSC, FDA, USDA, NHTSA, or USCG. Always present.

source_recall_id.title: Source Recall Id
source_recall_id.description: Each agency's own recall identifier; its form varies by agency (e.g. CPSC recall number, FDA event id, USDA's DDD-YYYY number, NHTSA campaign number, USCG recall number). Pair with `source` for a globally unique key. Always present.

title.title: Title
title.description: The recall's headline. CPSC and USDA provide one directly; for FDA, NHTSA, and USCG (which have none) it is built from the recall id and the firm or model name. Effectively always present.

url.title: Url
url.description: Link to the recall's public detail page. Sources: CPSC, USDA, USCG (null for FDA and NHTSA, which don't publish a per-recall page).

announced_at.title: Announced At
announced_at.description: When the recall was first announced or initiated. Null for about 20 FDA recalls that have no reliable announcement date; use `published_at` when you need a date that is always present. Sources: all five.

published_at.title: Published At
published_at.description: When the recall was last published or updated. Always present, and the key used for sorting and pagination (unlike `announced_at`, which can be null). For NHTSA this is really a record-creation date, since NHTSA does not publish a last-modified date. Sources: all five.

classification.title: Classification
classification.description: Recall severity in each agency's own scale (FDA: 1/2/3, NC = Not Yet Classified; USDA: Class I/II/III, Public Health Alert; USCG: H/L/M/S). Not comparable across agencies. ⚠ USCG's H/L/M/S are passed through from the USCG directory, but their official meaning is not publicly documented (the public USCG recall index shows no severity, and 33 CFR 179 defines none), so do not assume an ordered scale. Best current guess, pending confirmation from USCG: H/M/L roughly map to High/Medium/Low, and S is unverified. Sources: FDA, USDA, USCG (null for CPSC/NHTSA).

risk_level.title: Risk Level
risk_level.description: USDA's health-risk label, which maps directly to its classification (e.g. 'High - Class I', 'Low - Class II', 'Marginal - Class III', 'Public Health Alert'). Sources: USDA only (null for the others).

lifecycle_status.title: Lifecycle Status
lifecycle_status.description: The recall's status in each agency's own words (FDA: Ongoing/Completed/Terminated; USDA: Active Recall/Closed Recall/Public Health Alert; USCG: Open/Closed). Not standardized across agencies; see `is_active` for a single yes/no. Sources: FDA, USDA, USCG (null for CPSC/NHTSA).

is_active.title: Is Active
is_active.description: Whether the recall is still active, based on each agency's status (a USDA Public Health Alert counts as active). Null for CPSC and NHTSA, which don't track a status, so they match neither true nor false. Sources: FDA, USDA, USCG.

reason_category.title: Reason Category
reason_category.description: USDA's categorized recall reasons, comma-joined (e.g. 'Unreported Allergens, Misbranding'). Sources: USDA only; for the other agencies the reason is free text in `recall_reason`.

distribution_scope.title: Distribution Scope
distribution_scope.description: How widely the product was distributed, always present: Nationwide, International, Regional, or Unspecified. Derived from distribution text for FDA and USDA; CPSC and USCG default to Unspecified, NHTSA to Nationwide. Sources: all five.

primary_firm_name.title: Primary Firm Name
primary_firm_name.description: The recall's main firm, chosen by role priority (manufacturer, then establishment, filer, importer, distributor, then alphabetical). Null only when no firm could be matched. Sources: all five.

firm_count.title: Firm Count
firm_count.description: Number of distinct firms linked to this recall across all roles. A firm with several roles counts once, so this can be less than the number of entries in `firms`. 0 when none matched. Sources: all five.

product_count.title: Product Count
product_count.description: Number of distinct products on this recall. CPSC, FDA, and NHTSA can have several; USDA and USCG always have one. Never null. Sources: all five.

has_been_edited.title: Has Been Edited
has_been_edited.description: True if the pipeline has spotted at least one meaningful change to a tracked field (recall reason, classification, status, title, or termination date) since it began tracking this recall. It is evidence of an observed edit, not an official agency amendment, and it carries no date. Present for all sources.

RecallSummary.title: RecallSummary
RecallSummary.description: A recall as it appears in list results (the commonly used fields, not the full record).

Source.title: Source
Source.description: The issuing agency: CPSC, FDA, USDA, NHTSA, or USCG.

sources.title: Sources
sources.description: The agencies covered: CPSC, FDA, USDA, NHTSA, USCG.

last_rebuilt_at.title: Last Rebuilt At
last_rebuilt_at.description: When the data was last rebuilt (UTC).

StatsOverview.title: StatsOverview
StatsOverview.description: Headline totals for an overview or landing page.

StatsSource.title: StatsSource
StatsSource.description:
An agency, plus ``ALL`` for the all-agency rollup.

Not every endpoint uses every value: monthly-trend has no ``ALL``; by-country covers FDA and
USDA; units covers NHTSA, USCG, FDA, and USDA with no ``ALL`` and no CPSC. A value with no data
for a given stat returns an empty list.

status.title: Status
status.description: 'active', 'inactive', or 'unknown'. CPSC and NHTSA have no status, so they are 'unknown'.

StatusCount.title: StatusCount
StatusCount.description: Active, inactive, and unknown recall counts per agency, plus an ``ALL`` rollup.

source.title: Source
source.description: NHTSA and USCG report units affected; FDA and USDA report quantity. No `ALL` rollup.

unit_category.title: Unit Category
unit_category.description: 'count', 'weight', 'volume', or 'grouping'. Keeps incomparable units apart; never add across categories or agencies.

total_units.title: Total Units
total_units.description: Sum of the per-recall amounts (a measure of recall size, not a count of unique items).

UnitsRow.title: UnitsRow
UnitsRow.description: Units recalled per agency, unit type, and month. Not comparable across agencies.

status.title: Status
status.description: The boat builder's operating status, passed through from the USCG directory. Values seen so far: 'In Business', 'Inactive', 'Federal or State Agency'. Not restricted, so a new value from the source still parses. Sources: USCG only.

UscgManufacturer.title: UscgManufacturer
UscgManufacturer.description: A USCG boat-builder registration record for the firm.

UsdaEstablishment.title: UsdaEstablishment
UsdaEstablishment.description: A USDA establishment registration record for the firm.
